paper_title: Evaluation of the oxidation stability of jatropha biodiesel/diesel blends

University located in Vietnam.
Research center located in Japan.
The primary field of research concerning renewable energy sources for transportation.
The susceptibility of biodiesel to oxidation due to unsaturated fatty acid chains.
The process of creating fuel from vegetable oils or animal fats.
A chemical process used to produce Jatropha methyl ester (JME) from Jatropha curcas L. oil.
A process involving sulfuric acid to prepare crude oils with high free fatty acids.
Analytical method used to identify fatty acid composition.
Standard test method for determining the oxidation stability of biodiesel blends.
A test to judge the corrosiveness of a fuel and its impact on fuel quality.
Chemical solution used in the transesterification process.
Method using bubbling oxygen at elevated temperatures to evaluate fuel stability.
